replies.msg:
I am afraid you did not understand what I say
We are not categorising at our will
We are talking about the ones who voluntarily affiliate themselves with Antifa
So the classification you made is irrelevant

As to why I attribute all definitions together, I wish to draw an analogy from the concept of joint criminal enterprise (you may read HKSAR v Chan Kam Shing [2016] HKCFA 87 https://www.hklii.hk/cgi-bin/sinodisp/eng/hk/cases/hkcfa/2016/87.html?stem=&synonyms=&query=Chan%2520kam%2520shing%2520FACC%25205/2016 ) where a person who foresees a risk (assuming not remote) of an additional offence to be committed by the joint enterprise (sharing a common purpose) in the course of executing that purpose, and nevertheless participates in the joint enterprise, would result in an implied authorisation and therefore forms the necessary mental state for committing the additional offence... When the act or omission of the additional offence has been committed by anyone else in the joint enterprise then the person concerned will be liable for that offence

The analogy can be drawn to make inference that the required mental state exist when they know that some people in the group might commit homicide, loot or damage property... And nevertheless they gave support to Antifa by saying they are part of Antifa, then it is safe to attribute whatever vicious acts to them and (morally not legally) must have agreed to whatever values Antifa could be associated with

I hope you would understand why we don't distinguish using the way you proposed... Because it is more sound to classify them based on voluntary participation
